mission.title: Install llm-d Precise Prefix Cache Routing
mission.description: Deploy the precise prefix cache aware routing feature for llm-d, which uses vLLM KV-Events data to pull up-to-date prefix cache status directly from serving instances. This eliminates the need for additional indexing services and increases cache hit rate at high throughput compared to the approximate prefix cache scorer used in the standard inference scheduling path.

steps.title: Install prerequisite client tools
steps.description:
Ensure you have:
- `helm` (v3+)
- `helmfile` (v0.169+)
- `kubectl` (v1.29+)
- `yq` (v4+)

See the [client setup guide](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/prereq/client-setup).

steps.title: Configure cluster and prerequisites
steps.description:
Ensure your cluster has:
1. 16 GPUs (8 replicas x 2 GPUs each) — NVIDIA or Intel XPU supported
2. Gateway API CRDs and a gateway provider deployed
3. Monitoring stack installed

See [infrastructure prerequisites](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/prereq/infrastructure).

steps.title: Create namespace and HuggingFace token secret
steps.description:
```bash
export NAMESPACE=llm-d-precise
kubectl create namespace ${NAMESPACE}

kubectl create secret generic llm-d-hf-token \
  --from-literal=HF_TOKEN=<your-hf-token> \
  -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

steps.title: Deploy the stack
steps.description:
```bash
cd guides/precise-prefix-cache-aware
helmfile apply -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

**Pod Discovery Mode** (experimental, for active-active multi-scheduler):
```bash
POD_DISCOVERY=true helmfile apply -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

**Intel XPU deployment:**
```bash
helmfile apply -e xpu -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

**For specific gateway providers:**
```bash
helmfile apply -e kgateway -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

steps.title: Install HTTPRoute
steps.description:
**For Istio or kgateway:**
```bash
kubectl apply -f httproute.yaml -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

**For GKE:**
```bash
kubectl apply -f httproute.gke.yaml -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check Helm releases:
```bash
helm list -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

Expected: `gaie-kv-events`, `infra-kv-events`, `ms-kv-events` all deployed.

Verify pods:
```bash
kubectl get all -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

Expected: 1 EPP pod (with KV-Events ZMQ endpoint on port 5600), 1 gateway pod, 8 model service decode pods.

steps.title: Test precise prefix cache scoring
steps.description:
Send an inference request, then repeat it to verify cache scoring:
```bash
kubectl port-forward -n ${NAMESPACE} service/infra-kv-events-inference-gateway-istio 8000:80

curl -s http://localhost:8000/v1/completions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model": "Qwen/Qwen3-32B", "prompt": "Hello world", "max_tokens": 50}' | jq
```

Check scorer logs (repeat the request to see non-zero scores):
```bash
kubectl logs -l inferencepool=gaie-kv-events-epp -n ${NAMESPACE} --tail 100 | grep "Calculated score" | grep "precise-prefix-cache-scorer"
```

resolution.summary: A successful deployment shows 3 Helm releases (gaie-kv-events, infra-kv-events, ms-kv-events) and all pods running. The precise prefix cache scorer returns non-zero scores for repeated requests with shared prefixes, indicating cache hits.

uninstall.title: Destroy the helmfile deployment

upgrade.title: Update llm-d version
upgrade.description: Update the llm-d version in your helmfile configuration.

upgrade.title: Verify cache scoring
upgrade.description: After upgrade, test that precise prefix cache scoring still works by sending repeated requests and checking scorer logs.

troubleshooting.title: Scorer always returns score 0
troubleshooting.description: Score 0 is expected for the first request with a given prefix. Send the same request again — the second time should show non-zero scores for the pod that cached the KV blocks. Logs only appear for unique requests.

troubleshooting.title: KV-Events not being received
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the EPP service exposes port 5600 for ZMQ:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n ${NAMESPACE} | grep epp
```

Check EPP logs for KV-Event connection issues:
```bash
kubectl logs -l inferencepool=gaie-kv-events-epp -n ${NAMESPACE} --tail=50
```

troubleshooting.title: Pod Discovery Mode issues
troubleshooting.description:
In pod discovery mode, each vLLM pod publishes on `tcp://*:5557`. Verify the port is accessible:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <vllm-pod> -n ${NAMESPACE} | grep 5557
```

Ensure network policies allow communication between EPP and vLLM pods.

prerequisites.description: A Kubernetes cluster with 16 GPUs (NVIDIA or Intel XPU, configurable to fewer), Gateway API CRDs, a gateway provider, monitoring stack, and a HuggingFace token.
